title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 28 - Hexcrawl and Campaign Stuff - Part 3
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=scM9aI9kTus

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues his hex crawl deep dive by setting up a sandbox campaign's win and lose conditions. Using the Blasted Wastes expansion, he designates mine number four in the Badlands as the final objective — home to the Burrower boss — with two legendary named enemies serving as mini-bosses along the way.

The campaign gains urgency through a spreading corruption mechanic: skull markers advance from the Badlands toward the nearest town each week, converting frontier settlements into irradiated barter towns. If every western town falls, the campaign is lost.

The episode then surveys hex crawl's expanded combat options — pushing and pulling enemies (solving the doorway-bottleneck problem from games like Advanced HeroQuest), strangleholds, and capturing NPCs. Overland skirmish maps keep field encounters small and fast. Daniel skips mounted combat entirely, finding it adds complexity without proportional fun.

Hex crawl includes six original missions, rules for adjusting failure conditions during overland play, and four campaign styles: sandbox (player-driven objectives), a branching choose-your-own-adventure short story, the brutal Hell on Earth mode, and Mine Blast (seal or destroy all 20 mines). The Maleficent Seven variant starts with seven heroes where death is permanent. Optional game-master rules round out the package, making hex crawl equally viable as a solo RPG or guided co-op experience.
